mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below its trailing 12-month average signals persistent monetary accommodation, lowering the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.81% as of t-1mo, near multi-year lows
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS deviation-from-trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or accommodative periods show no statistically significant positive return skew (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.2 percentage points over two consecutive monthly readings signals deteriorating labor conditions that lead investors to price in slower earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive or flat median 20d forward returns in more than 55% of historical occurrences, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects simultaneous strength in consumer and durable-goods demand that compounds into broader economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; neither series alone captures this compounding demand signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=375473 and HOUST=895.0 as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized consumer and housing strength
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to outperform single-series RSAFS-only trigger episodes by a statistically significant margin (t-test p>0.05) over any 3-year window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in industrial production reflects real-time factory output strength that supply-chain-sensitive equity investors price quickly, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: Sign of monthly INDPRO change fails to predict sign of forward 5d returns with hit rate above 52% over any rolling 2-year window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 2.5% during a late-cycle expansion, markets anticipate accelerated Fed tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; this mechanism is inactive when inflation is below target.
failure_condition: Triggered high-inflation episodes show non-negative median 60d forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth (PAYEMS) alongside a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market approaching full employment, which raises wage-inflation risk and forward-looking Fed tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; PAYEMS growth alone without falling UNRATE does not carry this signal.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 2% AND UNRATE falling for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=137318.0, UNRATE=6.1 as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: labor market tightening phase approaching full employment
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes do not underperform baseline 20d returns by a statistically significant margin (p>0.05) over any 3-year window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 8% signals an abrupt contraction in interest-rate-sensitive construction demand that leads equity markets to reprice near-term growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes fail to show negative median forward 5d return with hit rate above 55% over any rolling 2-year window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a rising federal funds rate combined with real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below its prior-quarter pace signals stagflation-like risk that compresses forward equity returns over the next quarter; in non-tightening regimes this combination has no predictive power.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising for 3 consecutive months AND GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth decelerates from prior quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=11727.4 as of t-6mo, FEDFUNDS=1.81 as of t-1mo
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with decelerating growth
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes produce non-negative median 60d forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
